Evidence Floor (summary): External or temporal validation relevant to the deployment population [9]; Calibration assessment beyond discrimination [10]; Defined statement of intended use and clinical actionability
Subgroup performance evaluation across socially salient characteristics [12]; Explicit statements about training-data representativeness [13]; Defined governance response for detected inequities
Model card or model facts label describing data provenance, exclusion criteria, and known risks [15,16]; Rigorous version control
Named accountable owner with explicit suspension authority; Separation between procurement incentives and deployment authorisation [18]
Predefined metrics covering performance, calibration, and alert fatigue; Prespecified thresholds triggering formal review, pause, or withdrawal
